name: Email Attachment with Executable Hidden in Double File Extensions
notes:
This rule triggers when a mail attachment's name contains at least two consecutive file extensions, and where one of them is associated to an executable.

This covers the case where an attacker appends a non-malicious file extension to the end of a malicious executable to bypass security services that block executable mail extensions. (e.g. virus.exe.txt, presentation.bat.pptx)

It also covers the case where an attacker sends a file with a non-executable file extension followed by an executable file extension.
This can be used to trick users into opening malicious executables, as some operating systems like Windows hide the file extension when displaying files in their file system explorer. If the attacker can convince a user to download the file 'Report.doc.js', the operating system may display it as 'Report.doc'. (e.g. report.doc.js, newsletter.pdf.exe).
conditions_text: when an event matches any of the following BB:DeviceDefinition: Mail
when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Content Information
when the event matches Filename (custom) is not N/A
when an event matches any of the following Email Attachment with Executable

when the event matches REFERENCESETCONTAINS('Executable Extensions',REPLACEALL('.*\.(.+?)\..+?$',"filename",'$1')) OR REFERENCESETCONTAINS('Executable Extensions',REPLACEALL('.*\.(.+?)$',"filename",'$1')) AQL filter query
TD_list: APPLY Email Attachment with Executable Hidden in Double File Extensions on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when an event matches any of the following BB:DeviceDefinition: Mail
AND when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Content Information
AND when the event matches Filename (custom) is not N/A
AND NOT when an event matches any of the following Email Attachment with Executable

AND when the event matches REFERENCESETCONTAINS('Executable Extensions',REPLACEALL('.*\.(.+?)\..+?$',"filename",'$1')) OR REFERENCESETCONTAINS('Executable Extensions',REPLACEALL('.*\.(.+?)$',"filename",'$1')) AQL filter query
RED_list: Event name: Email Attachment with Multiple File Extensions Contains Executable
Event description: Detects when a mail attachment's name contains at least two consecutive file extensions, and where one of them is associated to an executable.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Recipients (custom)
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
